examples.title: BST Search Operation
examples.description: Recursive search in Binary Search Tree
examples.explanation: Search recursively compares target with current node and moves left or right. Each recursive call goes one level deeper, so time depends on tree height.

examples.title: BST Insert Operation
examples.description: Insert a new key into BST maintaining BST properties
examples.explanation: Insert finds the appropriate leaf position by comparing values. Creates new node at first empty spot maintaining BST property.

examples.title: BST Delete Operation
examples.description: Delete a node from BST handling all three cases
examples.explanation: Deletion handles three cases: leaf removal (simple), one child replacement (bypass), two children (find successor, replace, delete successor). Each case requires O(h) time.

examples.title: Check if BST is Balanced
examples.description: Optimized O(n) algorithm to check if tree is balanced
examples.explanation: Uses post-order traversal to compute heights while checking balance. Returns -1 if unbalanced, otherwise returns height. Optimized from O(n²) naive approach.
